title: Lantern Protocol II: The Separate Agreements
timeline: Year 1 after Region Six / Bound Flame
primaryDoctrine: Agreement is not consent when stacked into coercion.
coreQuestion: Can consent survive when authority is laundered through separate institutional agreements?
coreThesis: Someone in the chain agreed. That does not mean the person did.

canonDecision.reason: The merged main outline defines Book II as a 24-chapter structure. The older 25-chapter scaffold branch is treated as draft/reservoir material until intentionally promoted.

mustPreserve: Civic Mirror is useful, deferential, and humanitarian rather than villainous.
Mercy Bloc has a serious moral argument rooted in disaster response reality.
Kaito Ren is persuasive, not a cartoon profiteer.
Consent Markets expose class inequality and delegated optimization risk.
Naomi builds field doctrine rather than only objecting.
Mara proves authority laundering through records rather than conspiracy monologue.
Common Refusal saves something important but does not create a clean happy ending.
mustAvoid: Do not introduce AEGIS as a major player in Book II.
Do not turn Civic Mirror into Lantern 2.0.
Do not flatten NGOs, ministries, or relief workers into simplistic villains.
Do not make represented consent sound clean or easy.
Do not make Common Refusal a final victory.
